title: Everyone wants long-vacant Skyline building reopened, but city and owner can’t agree when
summary: A 362-unit former Skyline Apartments building remains vacant despite a court-approved settlement requiring the owner to complete renovations and open for rental by April 30, 2026. The city and property owner are in dispute over the timeline for reopening, indicating a breakdown in enforcement of the settlement agreement and potential housing supply implications for Syracuse.
department_refs: Department of Neighborhood and Business Development

political_signal.description: Settlement enforcement failure suggests accountability gap between city and private property owner; potential constituent pressure regarding housing availability and vacant property management in downtown/neighborhood revitalization context.
